hypothesis: Halving block_d from 64 to 32 halves the per-CTA [max_active_groups, block_d] FP32 group_acc LDS table width, so it might reduce LDS pressure and bank conflicts in the retained TileLang query-major compact producer.

supporting_prior.read: A related source-fed HSK BF16 path also rejected block_d32: S4096 regressed from about 5.0 ms to 8.5 ms, so this is not only a compact-partial fold artifact.

decision.read: D32 is correct and improves the tiny S128 case, but the S4096 target regresses badly. Halving group_acc width is not enough; the doubled D-block work and degraded MFMA/staging schedule dominate.
decision.next_kernel_work: Keep block_d64 and change how the 512x64 accumulator is touched: wave-private or lane-group pre-accumulation before LDS, striped/padded LDS layout to reduce bank conflicts, or a lower-level HIP/CK/TileLang schedule that accumulates several selected rows for the same group in registers before doing group_acc read-modify-write.
